title: Reflecting a point in the x-axis
problem: Reflect the point $A(4, -3)$ in the $x$-axis. State the new coordinates.

steps.instruction: Write the rule for a reflection in the $x$-axis.
Apply the rule to $A(4, -3)$: keep the $x$-coordinate and change the sign of the $y$-coordinate.

title: Rotating a point 180° about the origin
problem: Rotate the point $B(-3, 2)$ by $180°$ about the origin. State the new coordinates.

steps.instruction: Write the rule for a $180°$ rotation about the origin.
Apply the rule to $B(-3, 2)$: change the sign of both coordinates.

title: Identifying a transformation from original and image coordinates
problem: The point $P(-2, 5)$ maps to $P'(-5, -2)$. Identify the transformation.

steps.instruction: Test reflection in the $y$-axis: $(x, y) \to (-x, y)$.
Test a $90°$ anticlockwise rotation: $(x, y) \to (-y, x)$.

steps.working: The image matches, so the transformation is a rotation of $90°$ anticlockwise about the origin.

answer: Rotation of $90°$ anticlockwise about the origin
